entries.definition:
h–z–ʾ to mock, to scorn, to ridicule, to sneer at, derision; to
break; to perish, to cause death of cattle by allowing them to get
too cold. Of this root four forms occur 34 times in the Qur’an:
يَسْتَهْزِئ yastahziʾ 17 times; اُسْتُهْزِئَ ʾistuhziʾa four times;
مُسْتَهْزِئُون mustahziʾūn twice and هُزُوًا huzuwan 11 times.

يَسْتَهْزِئ yastahziʾ [imperf. v. X, intrans., pass. اُسْتُهْزِئَ ʾistuhziʾa]
1 to scorn, to mock, to deride, to make fun of (3:30) مَا يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ
رَسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ not a messenger comes to them but that
they make fun of him 2 (of God) to belittle, to have no regard for,
to show (someone) up as foolish (2:14–15) وَإِذَا خَلَوْا إِلَىٰ شَيَاطِينِهِمْ قَالُوا
إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِئُونَ اللَّهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ but when they are alone with
their devils, they say, ‘We are really with you, we were only
mocking’, God is making fools of them.

مُسْتَهْزِئُون mustahziʾūn [pl. of act. part. مُسْتَهْزِئ mustahziʾ] one
who scorns, scoffs, derides, mocks (15:95) إِنَّا كَفَيْنَاكَ الْمُسْتَهْزِئِينَ
indeed, We sufficed you against the mockers.

هُزُوًا huzuwan [v. n., variant reading هُزُؤًا huzuʾan] the act of

985

هُزُوًا huzuwan [v. n., variant reading هُزُؤًا huzuʾan] the act of

985
